yesterdayAnalysis: Final pre-race swim completed yesterday with 1.2mi open water at low HR (125 bpm), delivering 17 TSS. This was the planned confidence-builder in race venue conditions, allowing for wetsuit and sighting practice. The easy effort and controlled heart rate indicate proper taper execution with minimal stress added.
todayRecommendation: RACE DAY - Ironman 70.3 Oregon. Execute the proven conservative bike strategy from Oceanside: hold 145-152w NP (66-69% FTP), protect the hip, and set yourself up for a strong sub-9:10/mi run to the sub-6:00 finish. Trust your 13-week build and 13 brick sessions.
weekProgress: Week 13 of 13 (Race Week) delivered 94 TSS through strategic pre-race sharpening sessions. The taper was properly executed with easy course preview rides (18-20 TSS each), an easy 3mi run, pool work, and yesterday's open water swim. Volume and intensity correctly reduced to arrive fresh.
keyInsight: Sleep disruption on July 18 (score 34, only 3.4 hours, SpO2 90%) represents critical pre-race stress that may impact today's performance - focus on controlled pacing early in the race to compensate for compromised recovery.
